title: galeopsis tetrahit proles bifida
intro: Galeopsis tetrahit proles bifida, also known as Blue Foxglove, is a biennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparation of Galeopsis tetrahit proles bifida typically involves making teas or infusions from the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
